title: Golden Roasted Peruvian Chicken Stew
description: A resinous and flavorful Peruvian dish made with saffron and roasted chicken, cooked in a cataplana vessel. This weeknight stew is Buddhist-temple-friendly and features a glassy-jelly texture.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the chicken thighs with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in the cataplana vessel over medium-high heat.
Sear the chicken thighs until browned on all sides, then set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the cataplana vessel and cook until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chopped aji amarillo peppers and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, saffron threads, and browned chicken thighs to the cataplana vessel.
Cover the cataplana vessel and transfer to the preheated oven.
Roast for 30 minutes, then add the cubed potatoes to the cataplana vessel.
Continue roasting for an additional 20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the potatoes are tender.
